objective: Apply a fork-only long-term runtime fix that hard-disables FlashInfer by default (image + runtime) to stop recurring `flashinfer/jit ... gdn_prefill_sm90 exit 127` failures, while preserving a strict advanced opt-in path and Qwen3.5 guardrails.
scope: Apply minimal, reversible changes in this fork only to Dockerfile, docker-bake.hcl, src/engine_args.py, scripts/config_doctor.py, .runpod/hub.json, docs/runtime-compatibility.md, README.md, and aiwf.request.json.
acceptance_criteria: Build/runtime defaults hard-disable FlashInfer with deterministic `ENABLE_FLASHINFER=false`, ensuring flashinfer package is not active/available by default in the runtime image.
Runtime defaults still disable FlashInfer prefill deterministically via `DISABLE_FLASHINFER_PREFILL=true`, preserving explicit user opt-in only when image gate is enabled.
When flashinfer backend is requested but nvcc/ninja/C++ toolchain is missing, worker forces safe fallback and emits actionable warning.
RunPod hub presets and env metadata expose `ENABLE_FLASHINFER`, `FLASHINFER_TOOLCHAIN_READY`, and `DISABLE_FLASHINFER_PREFILL` clearly, with fork presets defaulting to safe disabled posture.
README and runtime compatibility docs capture root cause, hard-disable policy, and exact advanced override instructions.
config_doctor warns/fails on risky flashinfer opt-in combos when explicit toolchain posture is missing.
Validation gate commands complete: py_compile, config_doctor, JSON parse, and docker buildx bake --print.
tests_expected: python -c "import json; json.load(open('.runpod/hub.json')); json.load(open('aiwf.request.json')); print('json ok')"
rollback_plan: Immediate rollback is fork-local git revert of this runtime fix commit. Operational rollback without code revert is endpoint-level safety: set `ENABLE_FLASHINFER=false`, `DISABLE_FLASHINFER_PREFILL=true`, and `ATTENTION_BACKEND=FLASH_ATTN` to return to hard-disable posture. Advanced rollback for FlashInfer-enabled endpoints is to redeploy a previously validated image tag and preset known to include `ENABLE_FLASHINFER=true` with verified nvcc+ninja+C++ toolchain.
deploy_plan: Deploy fork-only in staged rollout (1->2->4 workers). At each stage verify cold start, successful model load, and one representative inference before scaling.
threat_model: Main risks are throughput regressions for workloads that benefit from flashinfer prefill and false confidence when users opt in without required toolchain. Mitigation: keep default safe fallback deterministic, preserve explicit opt-in path, emit runtime/config warnings, keep change set narrow/fork-local, and require staged startup+inference validation.
